mappings.rationaleAI: Religious fundamentalism across world religions as a reaction to modernity and secularization directly addresses new religious movements reflecting the impact of dominant cultures (A).
Alchemy's spiritual and symbolic dimensions, connections to Hermetic philosophy and natural magic, relate to residues or revivals of ancient beliefs and practices (F.1).
The Rapture as a 19th-century dispensationalist doctrine popularised through the Left Behind novels represents a new religious movement reflecting American evangelical culture (A).
Millenarian movements throughout history and secular apocalyptic narratives address prophecy and divination in modern contexts (F.2).
Gnosticism's modern revival through the 1945 Nag Hammadi discovery has influenced contemporary new religious movements reflecting the impact of ancient beliefs on modern spirituality (A).
